info.name: Rollout.io - Rest - v5.0.1
info.description:
Rollout.io Unified API

Merged specification covering all three microservices: Identity & Access Management, Control Plane, and SDK Evaluation.

  • Auth Service — User lifecycle, Firebase identity sync, profile management.
  • Control Plane Service — Feature flag governance, projects, environments, audit logs.
  • SDK Service — Deterministic flag evaluation, analytics reporting, SDK init.

All critical endpoints are protected via central IAM. A valid OAuth 2 Bearer token must be provided.

Contact Support: Name: Parthsinh Thakor Email: admin@rollout.io

exec: console.log('✅ bearerToken saved! Use {{bearerToken}} in all API requests.');
console.error('❌ Exchange failed:', json.error ? json.error.message : JSON.stringify(json));
console.warn('Make sure googleIdToken is set (run Step 1 first).');

request.description:
**STEP 2:** Exchanges Google `id_token` (from Step 1) for a Firebase JWT Bearer token.

Auto-saves:
- `bearerToken` — use as Bearer in all API requests
- `bearerTokenExpiry` — expiry timestamp
- `firebaseRefreshToken` — for Step 3 refresh

exec: console.log('✅ bearerToken saved via Email/Password login.');
console.error('❌ Login failed:', json.error ? json.error.message : JSON.stringify(json));

request.description:
**STEP 2 (Alternative):** Direct Firebase login with Email & Password. No Google OAuth needed.

Set `userEmail` and `userPassword` in collection variables. Auto-saves `bearerToken`.

exec: console.log('⏰ New token expires at: ' + new Date(expiry).toLocaleTimeString());
console.error('❌ Token refresh failed:', json.error ? json.error.message : JSON.stringify(json));

request.description:
**STEP 3:** Refresh expired Firebase token using the saved `firebaseRefreshToken`.

Auto-saves new `bearerToken` and updated `bearerTokenExpiry`.

exec: // Postman OAuth2 built-in flow stores id_token separately from access_token.
// We try multiple approaches to extract it.
// Approach 1: Postman v10+ parameters API (most reliable)
// Approach 3: Parse Authorization header — token is the id_token itself for Google+Firebase
console.log('✅ googleIdToken saved! Now run Step 2 to get Firebase bearerToken.');
console.warn('Manual fallback: Auth tab → Manage Tokens → copy id_token value → set googleIdToken variable manually.');

request.description:
**STEP 1:** Do NOT click Send.

Instead: go to **Auth tab** → scroll down → click **'Get New Access Token'** button → browser will open for Google login → after login Postman will auto-capture the token.

The test script extracts `id_token` and saves it as `googleIdToken`. Then run Step 2.
